metadata.description: Optional. Reverse the normal Cloud Adoption Framework naming convention by putting the resource type abbreviation at the end of the resource name.

Optional. Identifier used to describe the business unit (or customer) utilizing AVD in your tenant.
If not specified then centralized AVD Management is assumed and resources and resource groups are named accordingly.

Conditional. When the "businessUnitIdentifier" parameter is not empty, this parameter determines if the AVD Monitoring Resource Group and associated resources
are created in a centralized resource group (does not include "businessUnitIdentifier" in the name) and monitoring resources are named accordingly or if a Business unit
specific AVD management resource group is created and monitoring resources are named accordingly.
If the "businessUnitIdentifier" parameter is left empty ("") then this value has no effect.
Required. An identifier used to distinquish each host pool. This can represent the user or use case.
Optional. The target environment for the solution.
Optional. The name of the Azure Blobs container hosting the required artifacts.
Required. The storage account resource Id where the artifacts used by this deployment are stored.

Optional.
The resource ID of the managed identity with Storage Blob Data Reader Access to the artifacts storage Account.
If provided this identity will be used to access blobs. Otherwise, the managed identity created
by this solution will be granted \'Storage Blob Data Reader\' rights on the storage account.
Optional. The name of the blob that contains the PowerShell Az Module msi.
Required. The service providing domain services for Azure Virtual Desktop.  This is needed to properly configure the session hosts and if applicable, the Azure Storage Account.
Conditional. The password of the privileged account to domain join the AVD session hosts to your domain. Required when "identitySolution" contains "DomainServices".

Conditional. The UPN of the privileged account to domain join the AVD session hosts to your domain. This should be an account the resides within the domain you are joining. Required when "identitySolution" contains "DomainServices".
Optional. The name of the domain that provides ADDS to the AVD session hosts and is synchronized with Azure AD
Optional. The distinguished name for the target Organization Unit in Active Directory Domain Services.

The Object ID for the Windows Virtual Desktop Enterprise Application in Azure AD.  The Object ID can found by selecting Microsoft Applications using the Application type filter in the Enterprise Applications blade of Azure AD.
Optional. Determines if Azure Private Link with Azure Virtual Desktop is enabled. Selecting "true" requires that private endpoints are created for the global feed, workspace feed, and hostpool.

Optional. Applicable when "avdPrivateLink" is "true". The resource Id of the subnet to which the global feed workspace private endpoint will be attached.
If you provide a value, then the a private endpoint for the global feed workspace will be created on this subnet.

Optional. Applicable when "avdPrivateLink" is "true". "Enabled" allows the AVD workspace to be accessed from both public and private networks, "Disabled" allows this resource to only be accessed via private endpoints.

Optional. Applicable only when "avdPrivateLink is "true". Allow public access to the hostpool through the control plane.
"Enabled" allows this resource to be accessed from both public and private networks.
"Disabled" allows this resource to only be accessed via private endpoints.
"EnabledForClientsOnly" allows this resource to be accessed only when the session hosts are configured to use private routes.
Optional. The resource ID of the subnet where the hostpool and workspace feed (if applicable) will be attached.
Optional. Create private endpoints for all deployed management and storage resources where applicable.
Conditional. The Resource Id of the subnet on which to create the storage account, keyvault, and other resources private link. Required when "privateEndoints" = true.
Conditional. If using private endpoints with Automation Accounts, input the Resource ID for the Private DNS Zone linked to your hub virtual network. Required when "managementPrivateEndpoints is true.
Conditional. If using private endpoints with Azure files, input the Resource ID for the Private DNS Zone linked to your hub virtual network. Required when "storagePrivateEndpoints" is true.
Conditional. If using private endpoints with Azure files, input the Resource ID for the Private DNS Zone linked to your hub virtual network. Required when "storagePrivateEndpoints" is true.

Conditional. If using private endpoints with Azure Virtual Desktop, input the Resource ID for the Private DNS Zone used for initial feed discovery. Required when the "globalFeedPrivateEndpointSubnetResourceId" is provided.

Conditional. If using private endpoints with Azure Virtual Desktop, input the Resource ID for the Private DNS Zone used for feed download and connections to host pools. Required when either "feedPrivateEndpointSubnetResourceId" or "hostPoolPrivateEndpointSubnetResourceId" is provided.
Conditional. If using private endpoints with Key Vaults, input the Resource ID for the Private DNS Zone linked to your hub virtual network. Required when "managementPrivateEndpoints" is true.
Optional. Deploy the Zero Trust Compliant Disk Access Policy to deny Public Access to the Virtual Machine Managed Disks.
Optional. Determines whether resources to support FSLogix profile storage are deployed.
Optional. The custom prefix to use for the name of the Azure files storage accounts to use for FSLogix. If not specified, the name is generated automatically.
Optional. The file share size(s) in GB for the fslogix storage solution.
Optional. The type of FSLogix containers to use for FSLogix.

Optional. The storage service to use for storing FSLogix containers. The service & SKU should provide sufficient IOPS for all of your users. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/architecture/example-scenario/wvd/windows-virtual-desktop-fslogix#performance-requirements
Optional. Enable Automatic File Share Quota Increase for Azure Files Premium.
Optional. Configure FSLogix agent on the session hosts via local registry keys.
Optional. The name of the blob that contains the FSLogix Configuration Script.

Optional. Existing FSLogix Storage Account Resource Ids. Only used when fslogixConfigureSessionHosts = "true".
This list will be added to any storage accounts created when setting "fslogixStorageService" to any of the AzureFiles options. 
If "identitySolution" is set to "EntraId" or "EntraIdIntuneEnrollment" then only the first storage account listed will be used.
Optional. The resource Id of the Virtual Network delegated for NetApp Volumes. Required when fslogixStorageService = "AzureNetAppFiles Standard" or "AzureNetAppFiles Premium".
Optional. The Active Directory computer object Kerberos encryption type for the Azure Storage Account or Azure NetApp files Account.

Optional. The number of storage accounts to deploy to support the required use case for the AVD stamp. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/architecture/patterns/sharding
Note: Cannot utilize sharding with "identitySolution" = "AAD" so fslogixStorageCount will be set to 1 in variables.
Optional. The starting number for the storage accounts to support the required use case for the AVD stamp. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/architecture/patterns/sharding
Optional. The type of key management used for the Azure Files storage account encryption.
Optional. The deployment location for the AVD Control Plane resources.
Optional. The resource Id of an existing AVD workspace to which the desktop application group will be registered.
Optional. The friendly name for the AVD workspace that is displayed in the client.
Optional. The friendly name for the Desktop in the AVD workspace.
Optional. Enable drain mode on new sessions hosts to prevent users from accessing them until they are validated.
Optional. The maximum number of sessions per AVD session host.

Optional. Input RDP properties to add or remove RDP functionality on the AVD host pool.
Settings reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/windows-server/remote/remote-desktop-services/clients/rdp-files
Optional. These options specify the host pool type and depending on the type provides the load balancing options and assignment types.
Optional. The value determines whether the hostPool should receive early AVD updates for testing.
Optional. An array of Security Principals with their object IDs and display names to assign to the AVD Application Group and FSLogix Storage.
Optional. Determines if the scaling plan is deployed to the host pool.
Optional. The tag used to exclude virtual machines from the scaling plan.
Optional. The scaling plan weekday ramp up schedule
Optional. The scaling plan weekday peak schedule.
Optional. The scaling plan weekday rampdown schedule.
Optional. The scaling plan weakday off peak schedule.
Optional. Determines if the scaling plan will forcefully log off users when scaling down.
Optional. The number of minutes to wait before forcefully logging off users when scaling down.
Optional. The number of session hosts to deploy in the host pool. Ensure you have the approved quota to deploy the desired count.
Optional. The starting number for the session hosts. This is important when adding virtual machines to ensure an update deployment is not performed on an exiting, active session host.
Optional. Set the desired availability / SLA with a pooled host pool.  The best practice is to deploy to availability Zones for resilency.
Optional. The resource Id of the Dedicated Host on which to deploy the Virtual Machines.
Optional. The resource Id of the Dedicated Host Group on to which the Virtual Machines are to be deployed. The Dedicated Host Group must support Automatic Host Assignment for this value to be used.
Optional. The name of the blob containing the AVDAgent Agent installers and script.

Optional. Array of script (or other artifact) names or full uris that will be downloaded by the Custom Script Extension on each Session Host Virtual Machine.
Either specify the entire URL or just the name of the blob if is located at the fqdn specified by the [artifactsUri] parameter.
Optional. The name of the script and blob that is ran by the Custom Script Extension on Virtual Machines.

Additional Custom Dynamic parameters passed to CSE Scripts.
(ex: 'Script2Keys=@([pscustomobject]@{stringValue=\'storageAccountName\';booleanValue=\'false\'});Script3Keys=@([pscustomobject]@{intValue=\'10\'}')

Optional. Encryption at host encrypts temporary disks and ephemeral OS disks with platform-managed keys,
OS and data disk caches with the key specified in the "keyManagementDisks" parameter, and flows encrypted to the Storage service.
Optional. Confidential disk encryption is an additional layer of encryption which binds the disk encryption keys to the virtual machine TPM and makes the disk content accessible only to the VM.

Optional. The object ID of the Confidential VM Orchestrator enterprise application with application ID "bf7b6499-ff71-4aa2-97a4-f372087be7f0".
This is required when "confidentialVMOSDiskEncryption" is set to "true". You must create this application in your tenant before deploying this solution using the following PowerShell script:
  Connect-AzureAD -Tenant "your tenant ID"
  New-AzureADServicePrincipal -AppId bf7b6499-ff71-4aa2-97a4-f372087be7f0 -DisplayName "Confidential VM Orchestrator"

Optional. The type of encryption key management used for the storage. (Default: "PlatformManaged")
- Platform-managed keys (PMKs) are key encryption keys that are generated, stored, and managed entirely by Azure. Choose Platform Managed for the best balance of security and ease of use.
- Customer-managed keys (CMKs) are key encryption keys that are generated, stored, and managed by you, the customer, in your Azure Key Vault. Choose Customer Managed if you need to meet specific compliance requirements.
- Customer-managed keys (CMKs) storage in a premium KeyVault backed by a Hardware Security Module (HSM). The Hardware Security Module is FIPS 140 Level 3 validated.
- Double encryption is 2 layers of encryption: an infrastructure encryption layer with platform managed keys and a disk encryption layer with customer managed keys defined by disk encryption sets.
Choose Platform Managed and Customer Managed if you need double encryption. This option does not apply to confidential VMs.
- Choose Platform Managed and Customer Managed with HSM if you must incorporate double encryption and protect the customer managed key with the Hardware Security Module. This option does not apply to confidential VMs.
Optional. The storage SKU for the AVD session host disks.  Production deployments should use Premium_LRS.
Optional. The Security Type of the AVD Session Hosts.  ConfidentialVM and TrustedLaunch are only available in certain regions.
Optional. Offer for the virtual machine image
Optional. Publisher for the virtual machine image
Optional. SKU for the virtual machine image
Required. The resource ID for the Compute Gallery Image Version. Do not set this value if using a marketplace image.
Required. The resource ID of the subnet to place the network interfaces for the AVD session hosts.
Required. Local administrator password for the AVD session hosts
Required. The Local Administrator Username for the Session Hosts
Optional. The VM SKU for the AVD session hosts.
Optional. The vm size of the management VM.
Required. The Virtual Machine Name prefix.
Optional. Deploys the required monitoring resources to enable AVD and VM Insights and monitor features in the automation account.
Optional. The retention for the Log Analytics Workspace to setup the AVD monitoring solution
Optional. The SKU for the Log Analytics Workspace to setup the AVD monitoring solution

Optional. The resource ID of the log analytics workspace used for Azure Sentinel and / or Defender for Cloud. When using the Microsoft Monitor Agent (Log Analytics Agent), this allows you to multihome the agent to reduce unnecessary log collection and reduce cost.
Optional. The resource ID of the data collection rule used for Azure Sentinel and / or Defender for Cloud when using the Azure Monitor Agent.

Optional. Enable backups to an Azure Recovery Services vault.  For a pooled host pool this will enable backups on the Azure file share.  For a personal host pool this will enable backups on the AVD sessions hosts.
Optional. Key / value pairs of metadata for the Azure resource groups and resources.
DO NOT MODIFY THIS VALUE! The timeStamp is needed to differentiate deployments for certain Azure resources and must be set using a parameter.

You can find the GUIDs in the ID column on the table at https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/role-based-access-control/built-in-roles.

Optional. Name of the Resource Group to assign the RBAC role to. If not provided, will use the current scope for deployment.

Optional. The description of the role assignment.
Optional. ID of the delegated managed identity resource.

Optional. The principal type of the assigned principal ID.

The name of the resource group the role assignment was applied at.

source.script:
param(
          [string]$Environment,
          [string]$ResourceGroupName,
          [string]$SubscriptionId,
          [string]$TenantId,
          [string]$UserAssignedIdentityClientId,
          [string]$VirtualMachineName
        )
        Connect-AzAccount -Environment $Environment -Tenant $TenantId -Subscription $SubscriptionId -Identity -AccountId $UserAssignedIdentityClientId
        ## Introduce a wait here because every Run Command will require at least 20 seconds to complete. This avoids a condition where the VM gets deleted before the output of this command is returned to the deployment.
        Start-Sleep -Seconds 20
        Remove-AzVM -ResourceGroupName $ResourceGroupName -Name $VirtualMachineName -NoWait -Force
